_about: Machine-readable parity classification for expr-js vs expr-lang/expr v1.17.8. Generated from upstream test tables; Go is the source of truth.

notApplicableReasons.reason: Go fixed-width numeric types distinct from int/float64; JS has bigint+number only
Go pointer / typed-nil values have no JS analog
Go time.Time env value identity/formatting via reflect not modeled in JS mock env
Go fmt.Stringer/struct %v formatting (reflect) not modeled in JS

notApplicableReasons.pattern: has no field / has no method / is not callable / too many arguments / invalid operation / cannot get
notApplicableReasons.reason: Go strict-struct field/method checking via reflect; JS env objects are open maps (no closed field set or method signatures)

engineBugsFound: in over constant array (Set membership missing in runtime.In) — FIXED
env function rejected 'doesn't return value' (typeOfValue func had 0 outputs) — FIXED
duration * float returned duration instead of float64 (Multiply) — FIXED
